title: H2O2 Content Measurement in Wild Jujube Seedlings
author: Mingxin Guo
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.jqjcmun

steps.title: The seedlings were cultured in Kimura’s...
steps.instruction: The seedlings were cultured in Kimura’s culture solution B until six-leaf stage.

0.5 g of fresh root of wild jujube seedlings subjected to alkaline treatment of pH 9.5 for 0, 3, 5 days was incubated in 10 mL triphenyl tetrazolium chloride (TTC) solution (0.04% in pH 7.0 phosphate buffer) for 1 h at 37 °C.

steps.title: add 2 mL 1M H2SO4, the...
steps.instruction: add 2 mL 1M H2SO4, the red product in root was extracted using ethyl acetate.

steps.title: The absorbances were determined by spectrophotometer at 485 nm
steps.instruction: The absorbances were determined by spectrophotometer at 485 nm.

steps.title: The activity of the root system...
steps.instruction: The activity of the root system was expressed relative to the control value of 100%.
